changes.value:
## What

`swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs:320-324` hardcodes `get_daemon("rust-analyzer")` to grab a single shared client for the LSP indexing worker. Even when the supervisor successfully starts typescript-language-server, its client is never passed to any indexing worker. TS files get tree-sitter indexed but never LSP indexed.

The fix: after the supervisor starts, iterate all running daemons and spawn an LSP indexing worker per client, or refactor to accept multiple clients and route files by extension/language_id.

**Affected files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` (lines 300-330, 401-420)
- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lsp_worker.rs` — may need to filter files by language

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When typescript-language-server is available and started, TS files get LSP-indexed
- [ ] `mcp.log` shows LSP indexing worker connected to typescript-language-server
- [ ] Multiple LSP workers can run concurrently without DB conflicts
- [ ] Existing rust-analyzer indexing unchanged

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test verifying multiple workers can be spawned with different clients
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --package swissarmyhammer-tools`
- [ ] Manual: run sah in mixed workspace, check mcp.log for both LSP workers
LSP indexing worker: wire up all daemon clients, not just rust-analyzer
